title: The Hive
wikitext:
[[File:white.II.png|thumb|500px|center|link=]]
Access to this page is strictly restricted to The First, Impresarios, and M.E.G. designees only. Therefore, if you wish to access this page, you will be required to enter your valid credentials. You are currently being stalked and closely monitored by agents of the M.E.G. Under no circumstances should the contents of this file be disclosed to anyone without proper authorization. Any unauthorized reproduction, distribution, or public display of this document is an extremely serious offense and will result in the imposition of a maximum immediate execution as a consequence. You have been warned.

We have decided not to announce the specific reasons for restriction until further notice.

Notice
[[Level 45]] has been confirmed to be the only discovered safe and stable entrance to The Hive, and it has been sealed off by the M.E.G. Please be aware that the M.E.G. strictly prohibits the following types of groups from entering The Hive: *The group contains members under the age of 18; *The group does not carry sufficient weapons; *The group does not have sufficient food, water, etc; *The group is not equipped with protective clothing; *The group attempts to obtain information about The Hive; *The group is deemed unsuitable by the M.E.G. to enter The Hive for other reasons.





[[File:BF8CC94B-7A04-49AC-A763-F3192CB9201B.jpeg|center|thumb|800x800px||link=]]
fig 1.0ㅤ''One of The Hive's most recognizable images; photographer: James Bill, currently working for the M.E.G.''





Description
The Hive, also referred to as The Great Nest, constitutes an anomalous level within the Backrooms. It is widely considered to be the main habitat for a large number of entities. The Hive is an extensive system composed of horizontal caverns. It closely resembles Level 8 (formerly known as Area - 010). Due to this similarity, Level 8 was once regarded as a sub - area of The Hive. However, with the introduction and application of the [[Phenomenon 13|CBNS]], following a series of comprehensive analyses and evaluations, it has been firmly established that The Hive is an anomalous level with unique characteristics and independent properties. The structure of The Hive is exceptionally intricate and complicated. Navigating through it is deemed nearly impossible because the various locations are almost indistinguishable. It is highly likely for an individual to get lost in this environment. The interior of The Hive is filled with eggs from a multitude of entities, along with numerous wriggling tentacles. The insides of these eggs mainly consist of mucus, which can cause severe burns to the human body. This mucus can also be toxic, and if wanderers come into direct contact with it, they will die within three days without proper treatment. The eggshells are very fragile and can be easily broken if one is not cautious. The origin of the eggs in The Hive remains unknown. Up to now, no one has witnessed an entity mating in this location. Some believe that this is due to the vast size of The Hive, making the probability of encountering an entity during the mating process extremely low. Others think that the mating area of the entities is in a place inaccessible to humans. There are also hypotheses suggesting that the eggs within The Hive are generated naturally. Furthermore, there is a medium - thick fog that envelops the entire level. This fog leads to extremely low visibility, severely restricting the visual range of wanderers. Various studies and field observations have shown that this fog can cause significant mental harm to those within its range. Specifically, it can trigger hallucinations among wanderers. These hallucinatory experiences can further disrupt their cognitive functions and decision - making abilities, thereby increasing their risk. Moreover, this mist poses a potential threat to the overall health of wanderers. It not only affects their mental state but may also impact their physical well - being. Additionally, this dangerous fog has the ability to empower the entities in the area, making them more powerful and increasing the danger they pose to the wanderers. Fortunately, many areas within The Hive have extremely high levels of instability. This instability creates conditions that allow wanderers to no - clip. Consequently, finding the exit of The Hive isn't overly difficult, provided that the wanderers can withstand the harsh and dangerous conditions within. However, it must be emphasized that surviving in The Hive is an extremely difficult task, given the numerous hazards and threats that exist within it. The specific date when The Hive was discovered remains a mystery. The earliest available records related to it originate from The Lost, an ancient organization. They refer to The Hive as The Great Nest, depicting it as an inferno and a dwelling place for demons. According to their account, there should be an area known as the Queen's Chamber where something is locked up. However, up to now, the M.E.G. has yet to locate such an area.





[[File:The Hive.png|center|thumb|600px||link=]]
fig 2.0ㅤ''Another image of The Hive, showing the eggs of unknown entities.; photographer: Backers0121, a Backchat user whose real name is still unknown''




Entities
The Hive is populated by a diverse and extensive array of entities. Among these are the [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], [[Entity 5|Clumps]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], and [[Entity 6|Dullers]]. The reason for this is self-evident - as the level name suggests, this is the hive of all entities. The sheer quantity of these entities makes it extremely challenging for wanderers to survive in this level for extended periods. The constant threat posed by their presence creates a high level of danger and uncertainty. However, there is some good news. Many of these entities have only recently been born. As a result, their limbs are not yet fully developed. This lack of complete physical maturity means that they are not very fast in their movements and are also not highly harmful. This offers a glimmer of hope for those who may find themselves in The Hive, as it provides a potential window of opportunity to navigate and escape this perilous environment. Additionally, the mutation rate of entities in the level appears to be higher, which the M.E.G. speculates may be due to the shorter lifespan of the mutated entities, most likely having died before leaving the level.
The Tentacles
The tentacles within this particular environment is widely regarded as an entity that has a significant presence within the level. It is so extensive that it covers the majority of this specific area. It is hypothesized that the tentacles has its origin in a central region that, up to this point, has eluded discovery. Over time, it has continuously expanded and developed into its current state. There is a strong belief among researchers that the tentacles has a nutritional requirement. This conclusion is drawn from observations which show that it randomly seizes the eggs of other entities within the level through its tentacles and proceeds to consume them via its specialized mouthparts. It is of utmost importance to note that tentacles display a distinct preference for objects in motion. As a result, the likelihood of an individual or an object being caught by these tentacles is relatively high. Even in the event that one is fortunate enough to avoid being captured, the tentacle's physical strength poses a severe threat. Due to the tentacle's possession of an extremely large number of muscles, the force of its strikes can cause paralysis or even prove fatal to those in its vicinity. Food and edifices that make contact with the tentacles will experience accelerated decay. This situation implies that the establishment of supply points within this area is regarded as infeasible.





[[File:hsssssshiveI.jpg|center|thumb|600px||link=]]
fig 3.0ㅤ''A narrow passage inside The Hive.; photographer: Unknown wanderer, whose body was found along with this image in [[Level 1]]''




Colonies & Outposts
Establishing a long - term base within The Hive has been thoroughly evaluated and is unanimously considered to be an endeavor that is not only unrealistic but also entirely impossible to achieve. Moreover, it is an irresponsible course of action that poses an extremely high level of danger. Virtually all of the major organizations within the Backrooms, such as the M.E.G., the Backrooms Colonists, Kauer, among others, have made concerted attempts to set up a base within The Hive. However, every single one of these attempts has met with failure. We, therefore, issue a strong and urgent warning to all wanderers. Under no circumstances should any further attempts be made to establish an outpost within The Hive. Such an action is tantamount to courting death. The following is a list of some of the outposts that were once established within The Hive but have ultimately succumbed to the various perils and challenges present within this dangerous environment. These outposts serve as stark reminders of the futility and danger associated with attempting to establish a presence in this perilous location.
Maze Base. 【Established by M.E.G】 *Friendly and open to trading. *Has 10 explorers and 5 doctors. *Reason for the fall: All personnel are missing for unknown reasons. Spiderweb Safe Zone. 【Established by the Backrooms Colonists】 *Willing to provide resources to wanderers or allow them to rest in the safe zone. *Heavily armed. *Reason for the fall: Several members became insane in the level, and the rest fled in the chaos. Wormhole Outpost. 【Established by Kauer】 *For scientific research purposes only. *Heavily armed. *Reason for the fall: Loss of all personnel due to attack by hostile entities. Wildfire Outpost. 【Established by N.A.X.O.S.】 *The location of the base is unknown. *The exact number of people is unknown. *Reason for the fall: ''[ACCESS DENIED]''















































For the Kauer Thesis, our exploration has hitherto been rather superficial. However, it is of utmost significance to note that the theoretical potential within this thesis is truly unparalleled. Regrettably, due to our lack of in - depth attention, we have missed out on what could potentially be a highly plausible explanation for numerous strange phenomena prevalent within the Backrooms. The existence of The Hive can be effectively elucidated through the lens of The Kauer Thesis. In the past, our endeavors within The Backrooms were primarily centered around The Main Nine. Whenever we encountered something that transcended our comprehension during that period, our approach was to seek a simple yet reasonable explanation. For instance, if we happened to hear eerie laughter reverberating in the distance while in the midst of a desolate party, our subconscious mind would instinctively suggest the presence of something unknown. Subsequently, entities appeared inside the backrooms. So, the question arises: where do these entities originate from? The most straightforward explanation put forward was the existence of a hive of entities - a place that could be considered the home of all of them, the very source from which they emanate. This place was envisioned to be terrifying in nature, filled with entity eggs strewn all around, and thus, The Hive was born. Nevertheless, as time has progressed, we have established bases and outposts within the Backrooms environment. This has led to a significant evolution in our cognitive processes. Our minds have become more complex, and as a consequence, our explanations for phenomena that once eluded our understanding are no longer as simplistic as they used to be. Presently, The Hive is gradually losing its credibility and significance. With more and more people formulating new and diverse theories, the traditional notion that The Hive is the lair of entities no longer suffices to meet the demands of the human imagination. Inevitably, there will come a time when The Hive will cease to exist as a prevalent concept and will be supplanted by a more comprehensive and expansive theory, and it will disappear in the Backrooms as a result. However, the exact timing of this transformation remains uncertain. It could potentially occur as soon as tomorrow, or it might be postponed to a point millions of years in the future, or perhaps, this change may never come to pass at all.




Rewritten by [[User:所有盒子|Everybox]].
The original author is u/Nerdykiddo4844 and was previously rewritten by [[User:SherlsF|SherlsF]]
